title: Iconic-Group Bias in Vision-Language Models: predictions, metrics, and salience snapshots
description: Per-image classification predictions (Task 1; 30,000 records across five vision-language models and two prompt languages), per-image description outputs (Task 2; 5,000 records), per-cell aggregated metrics, a six-proxy Wikipedia salience snapshot, and 150 expert-rating records from the human-evaluation sub-study, supporting the paper Iconic-Group Bias in Vision-Language Models: How Salience Gradients Render Chinese Ethnic Minority Cultures Invisible (AI & Society, under review). The full source code for the evaluation pipeline is at the GitHub repository linked below; the 3,000 source images that constitute the benchmark are not redistributed for representational-harm reasons documented in the paper and the included DATASHEET.md.
notes: This deposit covers the derived data and the analysis-pipeline source code archived at the GitHub release tag corresponding to the paper. Per-image source URLs and integrity hashes for the 3,000-image benchmark itself are included via the bundled DATASHEET.md; the raw images are intentionally not redistributed.
